About Us

Your Essential Guide to California Civil Law

The California Civil Code provides the complete legal framework for civil matters across California, from property rights and contracts to personal rights and obligations. Our 2026 edition brings you the latest statutory updates and legislative changes, each division carefully structured for reliable legal research.

View Most Recent Edition

Our Mission

Helping attorneys, legal professionals, and individuals understand California's civil law with authoritative annual editions that reflect the most current statutory language.

Our Vision

To make our printed copies of the California Civil Code the go-to legal reference that practitioners and self-represented litigants turn to first when seeking accurate statutory guidance.

Our Values

We put accuracy first by providing verified, up-to-date statutory text — because accurate citations begin with accurate source material.

Why Choose Us

Understanding Built on
Experience & Care

All Civil Code Divisions

Complete coverage of every division, from property rights and contracts, to personal rights and civil remedies in three authoritative volumes.

2026 Edition

Our newest edition reflects the latest California legislative amendments, statutory refinements, and current provisions that took effect this session.

Systematic Organization

Structured from preliminary provisions through enforcement, designed for quick legal reference whether drafting filings or researching statutes.

The California Civil Code books are compiled by:

Jeremy J. Salvador

CPA · CVA · CFE · CrFAC · RFI · CFAE · CerFE · CAC · CCC · CTC
ChE · AMA · MFP · AFA · CFS · CFC · CIFA · ASBC

Jeremy J. Salvador is a testifying expert witness and Managing Partner of Miod and Company, LLP, where he has been specializing in forensic accounting for family law and civil litigation since 2009.


Mr. Salvador regularly prepares damage computations, business valuations, income available for support reports, arrearage calculations, tracings, community property balance sheets, apportionment analyses, and tax strategies.


He has qualified and testified as an accounting expert witness in the Los Angeles County, Santa Barbara County, and Riverside County Superior Courts, with extensive experience in civil litigation, forensic accounting, business valuation, and fraud investigations across a wide range of industries.
